reports.title: UK Electricity Mix Weekly Report: Saturday 18 July
reports.intro: A measured weekly report for EnergyMix.info using the available 7-day historical generation feed for Sunday 12 July to Friday 17 July.
reports.summary: The week from Sunday 12 July to Friday 17 July averaged about 24.7 GW of measured generation, with renewables averaging 42.5% of measured output. The strongest renewable day in the available data was Sunday 12 July, when wind, solar and hydro together averaged 58.7% of measured generation. Gas was highest on Thursday 16 July and lowest on Sunday 12 July.
reports.takeaway: For flexible electricity use, the cleanest opportunities are likely to appear when wind is strong, gas is low and demand is not at a peak. This is the foundation for future EV-charging guidance, clean-electricity alerts and weekly newsletter summaries.
drivers: Renewables were strongest on Sunday 12 July, when wind, solar and hydro averaged 58.7% of measured generation.
Wind was the largest swing factor in the available feed, peaking at about 7.0 GW average output on Monday 13 July.
Solar was strongest on Sunday 12 July, averaging about 5.3 GW across settlement periods after embedded-solar backfill where needed.
Gas averaged about 8.0 GW across the period, rising highest on Thursday 16 July and falling lowest on Sunday 12 July.
Measured generation was highest on Thursday 16 July, at roughly 26.9 GW average output.

cleanestPeriods.title: Best low-carbon proxy: Sunday 12 July
cleanestPeriods.body: Historical carbon-intensity aggregates are not yet part of this weekly report feed, so EnergyMix.info uses renewable share and gas output as cautious proxies. Sunday 12 July had the strongest renewable share at 58.7%.

cleanestPeriods.title: Lowest gas proxy: Sunday 12 July
cleanestPeriods.body: Gas generation averaged about 3.6 GW on Sunday 12 July. Lower gas periods are often cleaner, but the report does not yet claim this was the lowest-carbon period without historical carbon-intensity validation.

higherCarbonPeriods.title: Highest gas proxy: Thursday 16 July
higherCarbonPeriods.body: Gas averaged about 11.6 GW on Thursday 16 July. Gas-heavy periods are usually higher-carbon, but this is labelled as a proxy until historical carbon-intensity highs/lows are added to the report feed.

highlights.label: Wind
highlights.value: Highest average wind output was about 7.0 GW on Monday 13 July.

highlights.label: Solar
highlights.value: Highest average solar output was about 5.3 GW on Sunday 12 July.

highlights.label: Gas
highlights.value: Highest average gas output was about 11.6 GW on Thursday 16 July; lowest was about 3.6 GW on Sunday 12 July.

reports.methodologyNote: This report is generated from the available 7-day historical generation feed and validated against external public data. Carbon-intensity highs/lows and interconnector summaries should be treated as future additions unless they are present as validated historical aggregates.

yesterday.title: UK Electricity Mix Yesterday: Friday 17 July
yesterday.summary: Yesterday, Friday 17 July, measured average generation was about 24.9 GW. Renewables averaged 33.4% of measured generation, gas averaged 9.7 GW, wind averaged 3.4 GW and solar averaged 4.6 GW.

highestRenewableShare.text: Current measured 7-day high: 58.7% average renewable share on Sunday 12 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestWindGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 7.0 GW average wind output on Monday 13 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestSolarGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 5.3 GW average solar output on Sunday 12 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestGasGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 11.6 GW average gas output on Thursday 16 July, based on the available historical generation feed.

socialPosts.title: Monday weekly grid brief
socialPosts.body:
Last week in Britain’s electricity mix:

• Highest renewable share in the available feed: 58.7% on Sunday 12 July
• Highest average wind output: ~7.0 GW on Monday 13 July
• Highest average solar output: ~5.3 GW on Sunday 12 July
• Highest average gas output: ~11.6 GW on Thursday 16 July

The shift was clear: gas-heavy weekdays gave way to a cleaner, wind-led weekend.

Full report: https://energymix.info/reports/weekly/2026-07-18/

socialPosts.title: Wednesday explainer
socialPosts.body:
What does “UK electricity mix” actually mean?

For live grid dashboards, it usually means Great Britain’s electricity system: England, Scotland and Wales. Northern Ireland is part of the UK, but operates in a separate electricity market.

Explainer: https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix

socialPosts.title: Friday practical clean-electricity post
socialPosts.body:
The cleanest time to use electricity is not a fixed hour.

It changes with wind, solar, demand, imports and gas generation. Windy overnight periods and sunny middays can be much cleaner than still early-evening peaks.

Guide: https://energymix.info/cleanest-time-to-use-electricity
